page: Microsoft Azure M832ids_16_v3 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the MIDSHighMemoryv3 family. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors, this server features 832 dedicated virtual CPUs and a massive memory allocation of 15,200.0 GB. This configuration yields approximately 18.27 GB of RAM per core, positioning the instance as an ultra-high-capacity memory-optimized resource.

The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 410 GB of local disk storage. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-throughput external storage volumes. As an isolated instance, it provides dedicated physical hardware allocation to mitigate multi-tenant resource contention, though it lacks integrated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the massive scale of 832 vCPUs and over 15 TB of RAM, this instance represents a highly specialized hardware class designed for extreme resource density. The qualitative cost profile reflects its position as a top-tier enterprise resource where consolidation of massive workloads justifies the infrastructure scale. It is engineered for workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, massive enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data warehousing operations that require substantial memory footprints without GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure M832ids_16_v3 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine from the MIDSHighMemoryv3 family. Powered by Intel x86_64 processors and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance delivers 832 dedicated vCPUs and 15,200.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 18.27 GB of memory per core. The hardware configuration includes 410 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage for high-performance disk operations, though it does not include GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This server is designed for massive resource density, making it a qualitative fit for large-scale enterprise workloads. It is best suited for massive in-memory databases, large-scale ERP systems, and data warehousing applications requiring extreme memory capacity and high CPU core counts.
